nameMeanings.name: Hopton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Hopton, such as those in Yorkshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Shropshire, and Suffolk, from Old English hop ‘remote enclosed place’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

en.lifeSummary: Walter Hopton was born in 1255, in Hopton Bank, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son with Ela Herdeburgh. He died on 10 March 1305, in his hometown, at the age of 50.

ko.lifeSummary: Walter Hopton(은)는 1255년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 홉턴 뱅크에서 태어났다. 그는 Ela Herdeburgh(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1305년 3월 10일 그의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.
